summary: Implement the M7 phase-driven core action loop, obligation entry, and appointment feedback recovery
acceptance_criteria: Replace the current first-screen information dump with a visible phase/action loop that tells the player the current phase, the main unresolved decision, and the next primary action.
Task rail cards for obligations and appointments must open the exact handling surface directly, focus the relevant district or office/candidate, and explain why that action matters now.
The District 1 troop/obligation warning must have a discoverable handling entry point from the task card, inspector, and selected district context.
Appointment preview must clearly show before/after effects, cost/risk/benefit, and what will change after confirmation.

Appointment confirmation must have explicit submitted/accepted/rejected or pending state, disable or change the confirm action after submit, and make success/failure visible without relying on a small status sentence only.
Do not change authoritative simulation state outside existing command/query/read-model boundaries; no direct WorldState mutation from UI.
required_tests: focused React/Vitest coverage for task-card to obligation entry and appointment confirmation state
focused Playwright path covering first phase, obligation entry discoverability, appointment preview, confirm feedback, and no duplicate submission affordance
notes: This is a product-owner REQUEST_CHANGES recovery path. It must not approve formal M7 content lock.
The target is a playable, understandable low-fidelity product experience, not high-fidelity final art.
Manual node battle remains DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE and is not reopened by this recovery path.
No server, multiplayer, telemetry, paid service, account, cloud-save implementation, release, branding, or arbitrary-code mod scope is authorized.
UI must use read models and existing command/query boundaries; React, Pixi, and renderer code must not mutate authoritative WorldState.
All player-visible text must pass through existing i18n boundaries.
This is the first implementation task after the planning task is CLOSED; the lead should start it before any content-lock reconsideration.
